source: Industry standard references — NDS, ACI, OSHA, crane manufacturer data

lumber_properties.note: Fb = bending stress psi, Ft = tension psi, Fc = compression parallel psi, Fv = shear psi, E = modulus of elasticity psi. Values for No.2 grade.

dimensional_lumber_weights.note: Approximate weight in lbs per lineal foot at 12% moisture content

plywood_osb_weights.note: Weight in psf (lbs per square foot)

span_tables_floor_joists.note: Maximum spans in feet for floor joists at 40 psf live load, 10 psf dead load, L/360 deflection limit. Douglas Fir-Larch No.2.

mix_strengths.3000_psi: Standard residential: footings, foundation walls, slabs-on-grade
mix_strengths.5000_psi: Heavy commercial, bridge decks, water structures

concrete_reference.weight: Concrete weighs approximately 150 lbs per cubic foot (4050 lbs per cubic yard)

footing_requirements_utah.minimum_depth_below_grade: Frost depth per jurisdiction (24-42 inches in Utah)
footing_requirements_utah.minimum_thickness: 6 inches or equal to wall projection
footing_requirements_utah.rebar: #4 at 12 in OC both ways minimum for residential

crane_and_rigging_safety.note: Always verify with manufacturer load charts. These are general guidelines only.

rigging_basics.sling_angles: Never use slings at angles less than 30 degrees from horizontal. 60-90 degrees is ideal.
rigging_basics.safety_factor: Minimum 5:1 safety factor for wire rope slings
rigging_basics.tag_lines: Always use tag lines to control loads — never guide loads by hand

osha_crane_requirements: Certified crane operator required (NCCCO or equivalent)
Load charts must be in cab and legible
Outriggers must be fully extended on firm ground
Minimum 20 ft clearance from power lines (or de-energize)
Signal person required when operator cannot see load landing area
Wind speed limits per manufacturer (typically 20-30 mph max)

bolt_torque_values.note: Minimum bolt pretension per AISC Table J3.1 (kips). A325 bolts.

common_steel_weights.note: Steel weighs 490 lbs per cubic foot. Plate weight = thickness(in) x 40.8 lbs/sq ft.

osha_steel_erection_requirements: Fall protection required at 15 ft for connectors, 6 ft for all others
Column splices must have minimum 4 bolts before releasing crane
Perimeter safety cables at 42 inches within 2 days of decking
Controlled Decking Zone (CDZ) limited to 90 ft x 90 ft
Shear connectors (Nelson studs) must not be used as fall protection anchors
All connections must be secured before releasing crane load
Beams must be double-connected (minimum 2 bolts) before releasing

common_processes.SMAW: Shielded Metal Arc Welding (stick) — most common field welding
common_processes.FCAW: Flux-Cored Arc Welding — high deposition rate, good for field
common_processes.GMAW: Gas Metal Arc Welding (MIG) — shop welding primarily

fall_protection.trigger_height: 6 ft for general industry, 15 ft for steel erection connectors
fall_protection.toeboard: 3.5 inches minimum when tools/materials could fall
fall_protection.personal_fall_arrest: Anchor must support 5000 lbs per person or 2x expected force with safety factor
fall_protection.self_retracting_lifeline: Must limit free fall to 2 ft and arrest within 3.5 ft

scaffold_requirements: Base plates and mud sills required
Planking must extend 6-12 inches past support
Competent person must inspect before each shift
excavation_safety: Call 811 before digging — utility locate required
Trenches 5 ft or deeper require protective system (sloping, shoring, or shield)
Competent person must inspect daily and after rain
Spoil pile minimum 2 ft from edge
Ladder access within 25 ft of travel in trenches 4 ft or deeper

weight_calculation_helpers.beam_weight_formula: Total weight = weight per foot × length in feet
weight_calculation_helpers.example: W8x44 at 40 ft = 44 lbs/ft × 40 ft = 1,760 lbs total
